name: Sydney
summary: Sydney is Australia's biggest city, built around a huge sparkling harbour. It is famous for its Opera House with white sail-shaped roofs and the giant arched Harbour Bridge that crosses the water beside it.
funFacts: The Sydney Opera House looks like white sails or seashells, and its roof is covered in more than one million shiny tiles.
The Sydney Harbour Bridge is so huge that locals nicknamed it 'the Coathanger' — its big steel arch curves like one above the water.
Bondi Beach is one of the most famous beaches in the world, with golden sand and lifeguards who star in their own TV show.
Wild sulphur-crested cockatoos in Sydney are so clever they have learned to pop open rubbish bins with their beaks and feet.
Every winter thousands of humpback whales swim past Sydney's beaches on their long journey from Antarctica to warmer tropical seas.
thingsToDo: Visit the Sydney Opera House and walk underneath its huge white sail-shaped roofs.
Cross the Sydney Harbour Bridge on foot or by car for sweeping views over the boats below.
Catch a ferry across the harbour to Taronga Zoo to meet koalas, kangaroos and tigers.
Build sandcastles and paddle in the safe ocean pool at famous Bondi Beach.
Wander the leafy Royal Botanic Garden next to the Opera House and spot bright cockatoos.
Explore the old stone laneways of The Rocks, Sydney's oldest neighbourhood right by the harbour.

wildlife.name: Koala
wildlife.description: Sleepy grey marsupial that hugs gum trees and munches their leaves all day.

wildlife.name: Eastern Grey Kangaroo
wildlife.description: Big bouncy animal with a long tail and a pouch for its joey.

wildlife.name: Sulphur-crested Cockatoo
wildlife.description: Loud snowy-white parrot with a bright yellow mohawk on its head.

wildlife.name: Humpback Whale
wildlife.description: Giant ocean animal that swims past Sydney's coast every winter.

localWord.meaning: Good day, in Gadigal, the language of Sydney's First People.

images.alt: The white sail-shaped roofs of the Sydney Opera House at twilight.
The Sydney Harbour Bridge stretches over the water beside the Opera House sails.
Golden sand and turquoise waves at Sydney's famous Bondi Beach.
Sydney Tower rising tall above the city skyline.
A street in The Rocks, Sydney's old harbourside neighbourhood with sandstone heritage buildings.
Wide view of Sydney Harbour and the city skyline from Taronga Zoo.
A fluffy grey koala clings to a eucalyptus branch.
An eastern grey kangaroo grazing in the bush.
A white sulphur-crested cockatoo showing its bright yellow head crest.
A humpback whale breaching out of the ocean.
